ingredients.name: Biwajima Shrine
ingredients.address: 2 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small island shrine near Bocchi's home in Kanazawa-Hakkei that appears in episode 6 when she wanders the neighborhood alone, reflecting her habitual solitude before the band pulls her out of her shell.

ingredients.name: Harumiya
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-7 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The souvenir shop on Enoshima island seen during the band's episode 9 summer outing, capturing the lighthearted tourist energy that contrasts with Bocchi's usual anxious demeanor.

ingredients.address: 15-15-1 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The flower shop near Kanazawa-Hakkei Station in Bocchi's home neighborhood, visible in episode 1 establishing shots that ground her everyday suburban life before she joins Kessoku Band.

ingredients.entityContext: A bridge in the Kanazawa-Hakkei area featured in episode 7 when Bocchi travels back to her home neighborhood, serving as a quiet backdrop that reflects her introverted, solitary home life away from the Shimokitazawa music scene.

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒251-0036 Kanagawa, Fujisawa, Enoshima, 2 Chome−3
ingredients.entityContext: The treasury hall on Enoshima that the band visits in episode 9, part of their island pilgrimage where Bocchi navigates tourist crowds while trying to keep up with her more extroverted bandmates.

ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-19 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
